title: orthosia suberosa
intro: Orthosia suberosa, also known as the leatherflower or buffalo nut, is a perennial plant native to the southeastern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, due to the presence of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Orthosia suberosa include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
